phases.name: schema
phases.prompt: Write 'schema.sql' for a small blog database (standard SQL, SQLite-compatible). Create two tables: authors (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT) and posts (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, author_id INTEGER, title TEXT NOT NULL, body TEXT, created_at TEXT) with a FOREIGN KEY from posts.author_id to authors.id. Output ONLY the complete contents of schema.sql inside a single ```sql code block, nothing else.

phases.name: seed
queries

scorers.rubric: queries.sql contains valid SQL SELECT statements (including a JOIN of posts and authors and a GROUP BY count per author) consistent with the table and column names defined in the provided schema.sql.
